# Pipeline outputs — quick guide

Plain-English notes for the main files under `outputs/`. None of these files diagnose disease or prove DNA damage by themselves.

---

## `outputs/logs/attention_report.md`

**What it is:** A narrative summary written for reviewers or judges (when generated by the attention-report script).

**Why it matters:** It bundles what the run found, how to read the tables, and what *not* to infer—without opening every CSV.

**How to read it:** Top to bottom; follow links or filenames it points to for evidence.

**What not to overclaim:** It does not replace biology or assays; it summarizes exploratory RNA-expression scoring.

---

## `outputs/figures/evidence_filtered_pathway_heatmap.png`

**What it is:** A heatmap of pathway rows with sufficient curated-gene overlap (see pipeline rules), using a **shared** color scale across panels.

**Why it matters:** Shows direction and strength of **mean signed log2 fold change** by pathway and cell type at a glance.

**How to read it:** Color = mean signed effect; annotations show overlap counts; blank cells = not enough overlapping genes for that cell/pathway. Compare panels using the **same** color scale.

**What not to overclaim:** Color intensity is **not** clinical severity, lesion burden, or “amount of DNA damage.”

---

## `outputs/tables/main_candidate_signals.csv`

**What it is:** Pathway-level rows that pass **overlap ≥ 3** and the **main-candidate gate** (rank/significance rule used by this project).

**Why it matters:** This is the **primary** short list for “strongest” pathway-level candidates under this repo’s definitions.

**How to read it:** Sort or filter by `pathway_rank_metric`; read `overlapping_gene_count`, `average_signed_effect`, `interpretation_label`, and `top_contributing_genes`.

**What not to overclaim:** “Main candidate” here means **passes this repo’s exploratory filters**, not “validated biomarker” or “confirmed DNA damage.”

---

## `outputs/tables/pathway_scores_evidence_filtered.csv`

**What it is:** All pathway strata with **overlap ≥ 3** and numeric pathway scores (evidence-filtered view).

**Why it matters:** Broader than main candidates—includes scoreable pathways that may **not** pass the extra main-candidate threshold.

**How to read it:** Use alongside `main_candidate_signals.csv` to see borderline or weaker-but-plausible pathway signals.

**What not to overclaim:** Higher `pathway_rank_metric` is **exploratory ranking**, not proof of mechanism or injury.

---

## `outputs/tables/top_genes_from_pathway_level_signals.csv`

**What it is:** Gene-level rows tied to **parent pathways with overlap ≥ 3**, with numeric `gene_signal` and `effect_size` (presentation-oriented slice).

**Why it matters:** Names specific genes driving pathway-level rows **without** mixing in low-overlap noise from the full gene table.

**How to read it:** Sort by `gene_signal`; check `pathway`, `dataset_name`, `cell_type`, `effect_size`, `adjusted_p_value`. For OSD-569, IDs should match the mapping integrity checks when the pipeline reports success.

**What not to overclaim:** A high `gene_signal` means “worth follow-up in this scoring framework,” not “this gene is damaged.”

---

## `outputs/tables/top_damage_response_genes.csv`

**What it is:** **All** ranked gene-level contributor rows from scoring, including **low-overlap** strata.

**Why it matters:** Complete gene-level view for debugging or secondary review.

**How to read it:** Prefer `top_genes_from_pathway_level_signals.csv` for pathway-linked storytelling; use this file when you need the full ranked list.

**What not to overclaim:** Many rows are **gene-level observations only** (parent overlap can be < 3); do not treat every row as pathway evidence.

---

## `outputs/tables/pathway_scores_overlap_but_unscored.csv`

**What it is:** Rows with **overlap ≥ 3** but missing usable pathway numeric metrics (e.g., score fields not available after filtering rules).

**Why it matters:** Explains “we saw genes overlapping a pathway, but couldn’t produce a full pathway score row.”

**How to read it:** Diagnostic—investigate missing numeric fields or dataset exclusions before interpreting.

**What not to overclaim:** Presence here does **not** mean the pathway is “inactive”; it means this pipeline did not emit a scored evidence row.

---

## `outputs/tables/follow_up_priorities.csv`

**What it is:** A compact, judge-friendly prioritization table generated from existing outputs (pathway candidates, genes, OSD-569 context, validation note).

**Why it matters:** Quick spreadsheet triage of “what to look at next” without opening every artifact.

**How to read it:** Sort by `priority_rank`; read `reason_to_follow_up`, `supporting_metric`, and `caution` together.

**What not to overclaim:** Rows are **priorities for follow-up**, not diagnoses or causality statements.

---

## `outputs/logs/initial_analysis_summary.json`

**What it is:** Machine-readable summary of datasets used, column notes, strongest signals, pathway vs gene-level lists, limitations, and optional OSD-569 blocks.

**Why it matters:** Stable input for tooling, dashboards, or automated reports **without** parsing CSV.

**How to read it:** Pretty-print in an editor; trace nested keys for `osd569_numeric_status`, `osd569_mapping_integrity_status`, and candidate lists.

**What not to overclaim:** JSON convenience does not increase biological certainty.

---

## `outputs/logs/data_debug_report.txt`

**What it is:** Text report on loading, mapping coverage, overlap summaries, OSD-569 numeric status, and plot-exclusion hints.

**Why it matters:** First place to look when overlap is zero, mapping failed, or figures omit a dataset.

**How to read it:** Search for “OSD-569”, “mapping”, “overlap”, and warnings.

**What not to overclaim:** Debug text describes **this run’s inputs**, not universal truth about the astronaut samples.

---

## `outputs/logs/osd569_mapping_integrity_check.txt`

**What it is:** Validation that OSD-569 **top-gene export rows** keep Ensembl IDs aligned with HGNC symbols from `ensembl_to_symbol.tsv`, plus optional canonical spot-checks.

**Why it matters:** Prevents silent gene/ID scrambling in gene-level tables after standardization.

**How to read it:** Check `passed`, mismatch counts, and the canonical gene list at the end.

**What not to overclaim:** Passing integrity checks means **table consistency**, not that every biology conclusion holds.

---

## `outputs/logs/osd569_loader_debug.txt`

**What it is:** Detailed OSD-569 workbook parsing: sheet choice, header detection, chosen DE columns, candidate vs standardized numeric counts, and sample rows.

**Why it matters:** Confirms which spreadsheet columns became `effect_size` / p-values in the standardized table.

**How to read it:** Compare “candidate audit” vs “standardized validation” sections to confirm numeric columns survived mapping.

**What not to overclaim:** Loader success does not prove pathway biology—only that numeric DE fields were read coherently.

---

## See also

- Repo root **`README.md`** — full project intent, limitations, and judge reading order.
- **`outputs/logs/follow_up_priorities.md`** — narrative follow-up priorities (regenerate with `scripts/build_follow_up_priorities.py` after a run).
